qtype: number properties, place value, rounding, and order of operations
tex:
**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/31, Question 1]**

(a) Write the number six million and thirty in figures.
\newline

(b) Write 7.896 correct to 2 decimal places.
\newline

(c) \[ 8   24   25   36   39   41   48 \]

From this list of numbers, write down
(i) (d) a multiple of 16

    

(e) a factor of 24

    

(f) a cube number

    

(g) a prime number.
\newline

    

(h) Put one pair of brackets into this calculation to make it correct.
\newline
\[ 10 - 12 \div 4 + 2 = 8 \]

(i) By writing each number in the calculation correct to 1 significant figure, find an estimate for the value of
\[ \frac{596 \times 0.047}{8.65} \]
You must show all your working.
\newline

(j) Calculate $(8 \times 10^6) \times (3 \times 10^{-2})$.
\newline
 \\
Give your answer in standard form.
\newline

(k) $216 = 2^3 \times 3^3$ \\
Write $2160$ as a product of its prime factors.
\newline

content.content:
Write the number six million and thirty in figures.
\newline

Write 7.896 correct to 2 decimal places.
\newline
\[ 8 \quad 24 \quad 25 \quad 36 \quad 39 \quad 41 \quad 48 \]
From this list of numbers, write down
a multiple of 16
a factor of 24
a cube number

a prime number.
\newline

Put one pair of brackets into this calculation to make it correct.
\newline
\[ 10 - 12 \div 4 + 2 = 8 \]

By writing each number in the calculation correct to 1 significant figure, find an estimate for the value of
\[ \frac{596 \times 0.047}{8.65} \]
You must show all your working.
\newline

Calculate $(8 \times 10^6) \times (3 \times 10^{-2})$.
\newline
 \\
Give your answer in standard form.
\newline

$216 = 2^3 \times 3^3$ \\
Write $2160$ as a product of its prime factors.
\newline

tex:
**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/31, Question 3]**

Jack does a survey about cycling.
\newline

(a) The bar chart shows the percentage of people in each age group who use a bicycle.
\newline

(i) (b) Write down the percentage of people aged 60 to 69 who use a bicycle.
\newline

    

(c) 980 people in the survey are in the 30--39 age group.
\newline
 \\
    Work out how many of these people use a bicycle.
\newline

    

(d) Jack makes 18 cycling trips in one year.
\newline
 \\
Each cycling trip lasts 23 minutes.
\newline
 \\
Find the total time Jack spends cycling in this year.
\newline
 \\
Give your answer in hours and minutes.
\newline

(e) The table shows where 240 people cycle the most.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|}
\hline
& Number of people & Pie chart sector angle \\ \hline
Roads       & 84               & $126^\circ$            \\ \hline
Cycle paths & 72               &                        \\ \hline
Parks       & 48               &                        \\ \hline
Other       & 36               &                        \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
(i) (f) Complete the table.
\newline

(g) Complete the pie chart to show this information.
\newline

    

(h) A bicycle costs \$720.
\newline
 \\
Carlo pays one-fifth of the cost as a deposit.
\newline
 \\
He pays the rest of the money in equal monthly payments of \$16.
\newline
 \\
Work out how many monthly payments Carlo makes.
\newline

\newline

Write down the percentage of people aged 60 to 69 who use a bicycle.
\newline

980 people in the survey are in the 30--39 age group.
\newline
 \\
    Work out how many of these people use a bicycle.
\newline

Jack makes 18 cycling trips in one year.
\newline
 \\
Each cycling trip lasts 23 minutes.
\newline
 \\
Find the total time Jack spends cycling in this year.
\newline
 \\
Give your answer in hours and minutes.
\newline

The table shows where 240 people cycle the most.
\newline

Complete the table.
\newline

Complete the pie chart to show this information.
\newline

A bicycle costs \$720.
\newline
 \\
Carlo pays one-fifth of the cost as a deposit.
\newline
 \\
He pays the rest of the money in equal monthly payments of \$16.
\newline
 \\
Work out how many monthly payments Carlo makes.
\newline

tex:
**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/31, Question 5]**

(a) The scatter diagram shows the distance travelled and the cost for each of 12 taxi journeys.
\newline

(i) (b) `The scatter diagram shows positive correlation.
\newline
' \\
    Is this statement true or false? \\
    Give a reason for your answer.
\newline

    

(c) On one journey, the cost per kilometre travelled was much more expensive than on all of the other journeys.
\newline
 \\
    Draw a ring around this point on the scatter diagram.
\newline

(d) Draw a line of best fit on the scatter diagram.
\newline

(e) Another journey is 8~km long.
\newline
 \\
    Use your line of best fit to find an estimate for the cost of this journey.
\newline

    

(f) Arit, Luke and Marie share the cost of a taxi journey.
\newline
 \\
The cost is \$26.40.
\newline

(i) (g) Calculate how much Arit pays if they share the cost equally.
\newline

    

(h) They decide to share the cost in proportion to the distance they each travel in the taxi.
\newline
 \\
    Arit travels 12~km, Luke travels 3~km and Marie travels 7.5~km.
\newline
    \begin{subsubparts}

(i) Write the ratio $12 : 3 : 7.5$ in its simplest form.
\newline

        

(j) Calculate how much more Arit pays than if they share the cost equally.
\newline

        
    \end{subsubparts}

(k) Jin invests some money from his taxi company.
\newline
 \\
He invests \$18~600 at a rate of 1.7\% per year compound interest.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the value of the investment at the end of 6 years.
\newline
 \\
Give your answer correct to the nearest dollar.
\newline

content.content:
The scatter diagram shows the distance travelled and the cost for each of 12 taxi journeys.
\newline

`The scatter diagram shows positive correlation.
\newline
' \\
    Is this statement true or false? \\
    Give a reason for your answer.
\newline

On one journey, the cost per kilometre travelled was much more expensive than on all of the other journeys.
\newline
 \\
    Draw a ring around this point on the scatter diagram.
\newline

Draw a line of best fit on the scatter diagram.
\newline

Another journey is 8~km long.
\newline
 \\
    Use your line of best fit to find an estimate for the cost of this journey.
\newline

Arit, Luke and Marie share the cost of a taxi journey.
\newline
 \\
The cost is \$26.40.
\newline

Calculate how much Arit pays if they share the cost equally.
\newline

They decide to share the cost in proportion to the distance they each travel in the taxi.
\newline
 \\
    Arit travels 12~km, Luke travels 3~km and Marie travels 7.5~km.
\newline
Write the ratio $12 : 3 : 7.5$ in its simplest form.
Calculate how much more Arit pays than if they share the cost equally.

Jin invests some money from his taxi company.
\newline
 \\
He invests \$18~600 at a rate of 1.7\% per year compound interest.
\newline
 \\
Calculate the value of the investment at the end of 6 years.
\newline
 \\
Give your answer correct to the nearest dollar.
\newline

qtype: ratio, percentage decrease, and integer operations (temperature)
tex:
**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/31, Question 6]**

(a) This is a recipe to make ice cream for 6 people.
\newline
270~ml cream \\
    270~ml milk \\
    100~g sugar \\
    4 egg yolks
Tom makes ice cream for 10 people.
\newline

(i) (b) Work out how much milk he uses.
\newline

    

(c) The mass of all the ingredients Tom uses is 1100~g.
\newline
 \\
    After Tom heats the mixture, this mass reduces by 15\%.
\newline
 \\
    Find the mass of the mixture after heating.
\newline

    

(d) Tom lets the mixture cool to 5$^\circ$C.
\newline
 \\
    He then puts it into a freezer to cool to $-18^\circ$C.
\newline
 \\
    Find the difference in these temperatures.
\newline

    

(e) (i) (f) In a factory, a machine fills 25~920 tubs of ice cream in 8 hours.
\newline
 \\
    Work out the number of tubs the machine fills in 1 minute.
\newline

    

(g) A pack of ice cream contains 6 tubs.
\newline
 \\
    There are 120 packs on a tray.
\newline
 \\
    There are 24 trays on a truck.
\newline
 \\
    Work out how many tubs of ice cream are on the truck.
\newline

    

(h) Mario sells ice creams in five flavours.
\newline
 \\
The table shows the relative frequency of some of the ice cream flavours Mario sells.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
& Vanilla & Chocolate & Strawberry & Coconut & Banana \\ \hline
Relative frequency & 0.34 & & 0.18 & 0.12 & \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
Mario sells three times as many chocolate ice creams as banana ice creams.
\newline

(i) (i) Complete the table.
\newline

(j) One week Mario sells 450 ice creams.
\newline
 \\
    Find how many strawberry ice creams Mario expects to sell.
\newline

    

(k) The probability that any customer is an adult is 0.7.
\newline
    \begin{subsubparts}

(l) Complete the tree diagram.
\newline

        

(m) Find the probability that the first two customers are adults.
\newline

        
    \end{subsubparts}

content.content:
This is a recipe to make ice cream for 6 people.
\newline
\begin{center}
    270~ml cream \\
    270~ml milk \\
    100~g sugar \\
    4 egg yolks
\end{center}
Tom makes ice cream for 10 people.
\newline

Work out how much milk he uses.
\newline

The mass of all the ingredients Tom uses is 1100~g.
\newline
 \\
    After Tom heats the mixture, this mass reduces by 15\%.
\newline
 \\
    Find the mass of the mixture after heating.
\newline

Tom lets the mixture cool to 5$^\circ$C.
\newline
 \\
    He then puts it into a freezer to cool to $-18^\circ$C.
\newline
 \\
    Find the difference in these temperatures.
\newline

In a factory, a machine fills 25~920 tubs of ice cream in 8 hours.
\newline
 \\
    Work out the number of tubs the machine fills in 1 minute.
\newline

A pack of ice cream contains 6 tubs.
\newline
 \\
    There are 120 packs on a tray.
\newline
 \\
    There are 24 trays on a truck.
\newline
 \\
    Work out how many tubs of ice cream are on the truck.
\newline

Mario sells ice creams in five flavours.
\newline
 \\
The table shows the relative frequency of some of the ice cream flavours Mario sells.
\newline

Mario sells three times as many chocolate ice creams as banana ice creams.
\newline

Complete the table.
\newline

One week Mario sells 450 ice creams.
\newline
 \\
    Find how many strawberry ice creams Mario expects to sell.
\newline

The probability that any customer is an adult is 0.7.
\newline
Complete the tree diagram.
Find the probability that the first two customers are adults.

qtype: forming and solving algebraic equations related to volume
tex:
**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/31, Question 8]**

(a) 
The diagram shows a solid made from a cuboid and a cylinder.
\newline
The cuboid measures $x$~cm by $x$~cm by 4~cm.
\newline
The cylinder has radius 4~cm and height 7~cm.
\newline

(i) (b) Show that the volume of the cylinder is 352~cm$^3$, correct to the nearest whole number.
\newline

(c) Calculate the volume of the cuboid.
\newline

    

(d) Write down an expression, in terms of $x$, for the volume of the solid.
\newline

    

(e) Find the value of $x$ when the volume of the solid is 360~cm$^3$.
\newline

    

(f) (i) (g) Complete the table of values for $y = 2x^2 + x$.
\newline

    \begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
    \hline
    $x$ & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 \\ \hline
    $y$ & 0 & 3 & 10 & & & \\ \hline
    \end{tabular}

(h) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 2x^2 + x$ for $0 \le x \le 5$.
\newline

    

(i) Use your graph to solve the equation $2x^2 + x = 30$ for $0 \le x \le 5$.
\newline

content.content:
The diagram shows a solid made from a cuboid and a cylinder.
\newline
The cuboid measures $x$~cm by $x$~cm by 4~cm.
\newline
The cylinder has radius 4~cm and height 7~cm.
\newline

Show that the volume of the cylinder is 352~cm$^3$, correct to the nearest whole number.
\newline

Calculate the volume of the cuboid.
\newline

Write down an expression, in terms of $x$, for the volume of the solid.
\newline

Find the value of $x$ when the volume of the solid is 360~cm$^3$.
\newline

Complete the table of values for $y = 2x^2 + x$.
\newline

On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 2x^2 + x$ for $0 \le x \le 5$.
\newline

Use your graph to solve the equation $2x^2 + x = 30$ for $0 \le x \le 5$.
\newline

qtype: speed, distance, time calculations; average speed
tex:
**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/31, Question 9]**

Samir leaves home at 2~pm.
\newline
 \\
He jogs 6~km to a café at a constant speed of 8~km per hour.
\newline
 \\
He stops to rest for 1 hour.
\newline
 \\
He then walks back home at a constant speed and arrives at 4.36~pm.
\newline

(a) On the grid, draw a travel graph to show Samir's whole journey.
\newline

(b) Calculate Samir's average speed for the whole journey.
\newline

content.content:
On the grid, draw a travel graph to show Samir's whole journey.
\newline

Calculate Samir's average speed for the whole journey.
\newline

qtype: linear equation, indices, algebraic expansion and simplification
tex:
**[2024 Oct/Nov, 0580/31, Question 10]**

(a) Solve.
\newline
\[ 4x - 7 = 3 \]

(b) Simplify.
\newline
(i) (c) $(x^6)^2$

    

(d) $(5x^3y^4) \times (2x^2y^2)$

    

(e) Expand and simplify.
\newline
(i) (f) $4a + 5 - 2(a - 1)$

    

(g) $(d + 7)(d - 3)$

content.content:
Solve.
\newline
\[ 4x - 7 = 3 \]

Simplify.
\newline

Expand and simplify.
\newline
